overview: The Moses Text Filter processes InlineText files used by the Moses MT system. Each line in the file is read as a separate text unit, with inline codes represented by XLIFF-like elements (`<g>`, `<x>`, `<bx>`, `<ex>`) and line-breaks represented by `<lb/>`. The filter is typically used in conjunction with the Moses InlineText Extraction Step and Moses InlineText Leveraging Step for round-trip processing of various document formats through Moses.
processingNotes: If the document has a BOM, it is used to determine the input encoding; otherwise UTF-8 is used as the default encoding regardless of any specified default.
The output encoding is always forced to UTF-8.
The type of line-breaks in the output matches the original input.

Reading a Moses InlineText file may yield more text units than the original document because each segment (or unsegmented text unit) of the original is extracted as a single entry. Multiple segments from one original text unit become separate entries with no grouping information in the Moses file.

examples.title: Moses InlineText file format
examples.description: Example of a Moses InlineText file with four text units. Translatable text appears directly on each line, while inline codes use XLIFF-like markup elements (<g>, <x>, <bx>, <ex>). Line-breaks within an entry are represented by <lb/>.
examples.input:
Text in the first entry.
Text of the second entry<lb/>which spans<lb/>several lines
Third entry.
Fourth entry with <g id="1">bold words</g> and some code:<x id="2"/>
examples.output: Four separate text units are extracted, one per line.
